title: Flat-rate commission on total sales
problem: A real estate agent earns a commission of $2.2\%$ on all property sales. In one month they sold properties totalling $\$1\,350\,000$. Calculate the agent's commission for the month.

steps.instruction: Identify the commission rate and total sales value.
Apply the formula: commission $=$ rate $\times$ total sales.

title: Sliding-scale commission across three brackets
problem: A salesperson earns commission on a sliding scale: $2\%$ on the first $\$20\,000$ of monthly sales, $4\%$ on the next $\$30\,000$, and $6\%$ on any sales above $\$50\,000$. In June they achieved sales of $\$75\,000$. Calculate their total commission.

steps.instruction: Calculate commission on the first bracket: $2\%$ on $\$20\,000$.
Calculate commission on the second bracket: $4\%$ on the next $\$30\,000$.
Find the sales above $\$50\,000$ and apply the $6\%$ rate.
Add the three bracket amounts to find the total commission.

title: Retainer plus sliding-scale commission compared with a salary
problem:
Daniela works as a sales consultant. She receives a monthly retainer of $\$1\,800$ plus commission at $2\%$ on the first $\$30\,000$ of monthly sales and $4.5\%$ on any sales above $\$30\,000$. In a typical month, Daniela achieves sales of $\$55\,000$.

(a) Calculate Daniela's typical monthly income.

(b) A competitor offers Daniela a salaried position paying $\$48\,000$ per year. Which option pays more annually, and by how much?

steps.instruction: Calculate commission on the first bracket: $2\%$ on $\$30\,000$.
Find the sales above $\$30\,000$ and apply the $4.5\%$ rate.
Add the retainer to find the total monthly income.
Multiply the monthly income by $12$ to find the annual income.
Compare the annual commission-based income with the salary offer.
steps.working:
$$\$48\,000 - \$42\,300 = \$5\,700$$

The salaried position pays $\$5\,700$ more per year.

answer: (a) $\$3\,525.00$ per month. (b) The salaried position pays more, by $\$5\,700$ per year.
